prompt: Generate population density heat map of Canada, by referencing the style of the heatmap provided in the image.
prompt_refined:
Generate a population density heatmap of Canada. The map should emulate the visual style of the US population density heatmap in image 1.
-   Utilize a color gradient transitioning from light yellow/cream (lowest density) to orange and red (highest density) for population density, consistent with image 1.
-   Include a legend in the top-right corner, displaying 'Persons per sq. km' categories and their corresponding colors, precisely matching the ranges and appearance of the legend in image 1.
-   Depict major Canadian cities as white circles with black borders, labeled with their names.
-   Outline all provincial/territorial and sub-regional boundaries with clear black lines.
-   Present the map against a light blue or grey background, mirroring the aesthetic of image 1.
objects: Color gradient transitioning from light yellow/cream to orange and red for population density
Legend categories and corresponding color swatches (0.00 - 5.00, 5.00 - 10.00, 10.00 - 50.00, 50.00 - 100.00, 100.00 - 30,000.00)
City symbol (white circle) within the legend
Major Canadian cities depicted as white circles with black borders
Black outlines for provincial/territorial and sub-regional boundaries
